feed.title: Reddit Users Discuss Randall Preserve's Development History and Environmental Concerns
feed.summary: A Reddit thread on r/orangecounty recently featured a user inquiring about the history of the Randall Preserve, expressing surprise that the area was preserved rather than developed for retail/residential use. The user also raised questions about whether it was a toxic waste site and the influence of developers, indicating ongoing community interest and some skepticism about the preserve's past.

feed.title: Reddit Community Discusses History and Future of Randall Preserve
feed.summary: A recent Reddit thread on r/orangecounty explored the history of Banning Ranch becoming the Randall Preserve, including the significant $50 million donation from Frank and Joann Randall for its acquisition. The discussion touched on the area's past as an oil well site and ongoing efforts for tribal recognition, highlighting community engagement in its preservation.

feed.title: Randall Preserve Advocates Urge NMUSD to Sell Adjacent Land for Preservation
feed.summary: The Mountains Recreation and Conservation Authority (MRCA) and Randall Preserve advocates are urging the Newport Mesa Unified School District (NMUSD) to negotiate the sale of an 11.36-acre parcel adjacent to the preserve. The property, which currently juts into the preserve, contains sensitive habitats and species, and conservationists aim to prevent its development and incorporate it into the preserve's open space. Community members are encouraged to contact NMUSD to support the sale.

feed.title: Single-Family Home Under Contract in Banning Ranch Area of Newport Beach
feed.summary: A single-family home located at 22 Sand Dollar Court, within the Banning Ranch area of Newport Beach, California, was recently listed as 'Under Contract'. The property, featuring 3 bedrooms and 2 bathrooms, was listed for $950,000, indicating ongoing real estate activity in the vicinity.
feed.source: Sotheby's International Realty / eXp Realty of California Inc.

feed.title: Randall Preserve Seeks Stewardship Technician for Ongoing Operations
feed.summary: The Nature Conservancy is actively recruiting for a Randall Preserve Stewardship Technician. This role involves leading work teams for habitat restoration, managing ecological land and infrastructure, and interacting with community stakeholders, ensuring the implementation of the preserve management plan. The position signifies ongoing efforts in preserve operations and conservation programs.
feed.source: The Nature Conservancy / Conservation Careers / JobLeads.com

feed.title: Randall Preserve Advisory Committee Reviews Draft Recreation and Open Space Plan
feed.summary: The Randall Preserve Advisory Committee recently convened to review and discuss a draft recreation and open space plan for the preserve. This plan details proposed public access, including seven miles of trails designed to protect diverse wetland wildlife while opening pristine outdoor spaces to the public. Plans also include connecting Talbert Regional Park to enhance regional accessibility.
